title: Card payments timed out during checkout
summary: Customers could not complete card payments because payment-service calls to the external provider timed out.
rootCause: External payment provider latency exceeded payment-service timeout thresholds.
remediation: Check payment provider status page and latency metrics
Inspect payment-service timeout and retry logs
Temporarily route traffic to fallback provider if available

title: Reporting queries saturated database CPU
summary: Response time for /payments/create increased because reporting-service long-running queries saturated PostgreSQL CPU.
rootCause: Reporting-service analytical queries put extra load on the database used by payment traffic.
remediation: Inspect top database queries and CPU metrics
Check reporting-service query volume and long-running query logs
Temporarily pause or throttle expensive reporting jobs

title: SMTP delivery failures delayed top-up confirmations
summary: Users did not receive top-up confirmation emails even though money was credited successfully.
rootCause: Notification-service had intermittent connection errors to the external SMTP provider.
remediation: Check notification-service SMTP connection error logs
Validate SMTP provider status and connection limits
Replay failed confirmation email jobs after delivery recovers

title: Authentication token validation returned 401
summary: Customers and merchants received 401 responses because auth-service rejected valid access tokens.
rootCause: Token signing key rotation propagated to token issuers before validators refreshed their key cache.
remediation: Check auth-service key cache refresh status
Compare issuer and validator key identifiers
Roll back signing key or force validator cache refresh

title: Kubernetes OOM kills caused repeated pod restarts
summary: Payment-service and checkout-service pods restarted repeatedly because containers exceeded their memory limits and were OOM-killed by the Kubernetes scheduler.
rootCause: A recent configuration change increased request concurrency without adjusting container memory limits, causing containers to breach limits under sustained load.
remediation: Check Kubernetes pod OOM events with kubectl describe pod and memory usage metrics
Review and increase container memory limits or reduce request concurrency settings
Add memory utilization alerts to detect containers approaching limits before OOM occurs

title: Expired TLS certificate broke external API connections
summary: External clients and partner integrations received TLS handshake errors because the API gateway certificate expired without automated renewal.
rootCause: The cert-manager ACME renewal process silently failed after a DNS validation configuration change, allowing the certificate to expire undetected.
remediation: Renew or reissue the expired certificate immediately via cert-manager or manual issuance
Fix the ACME DNS-01 challenge configuration and verify renewal pipeline end-to-end
Add certificate expiry monitoring with alerts triggered at 30, 14, and 7 days before expiry

title: Platform-wide latency spike with no single identifiable cause
summary: Multiple services across the platform showed elevated p95 and p99 latency simultaneously with no clear single root cause; the incident was eventually traced to a misconfigured network routing policy.
rootCause: A network routing policy change caused cross-zone inter-service traffic to route sub-optimally, adding latency to all calls that crossed availability zones.
remediation: Correlate the latency spike timeline with recent infrastructure or deployment changes across all services
Check network routing policies, firewall rules, and load balancer configurations for recent modifications
Review inter-service call latency broken down by availability zone to isolate the failure boundary
